title: Quiz: The Oracle Model
description: Test your understanding of quantum oracles and their role in quantum algorithms

questions.question: What is a quantum oracle?

options.text: A quantum computer that predicts the future
A black-box unitary that computes a function f(x) without revealing its structure
A measurement device for quantum states
A classical subroutine in a quantum algorithm

questions.explanation: A quantum oracle is a black-box unitary operator that computes f(x) without revealing how the function is implemented. Algorithm complexity is measured by the number of oracle queries.

questions.question: What is the action of a bit oracle (standard oracle) U_f on input |x>|y>?
questions.explanation: A bit oracle applies XOR: U_f|x>|y> = |x>|y XOR f(x)>. This reversibly encodes f(x) into the ancilla qubit.

questions.question: What is the action of a phase oracle O_f on input |x>?
questions.explanation: A phase oracle applies a phase: O_f|x> = (-1)^f(x)|x>. This marks solutions by flipping their phase without changing the computational basis state.

questions.question: How can you convert a bit oracle into a phase oracle?

options.text: Apply Hadamard gates to all qubits
Use the bit oracle with ancilla in the |-> state
It's not possible to convert between them

questions.explanation: Phase kickback: When the ancilla is in |-> = (|0> - |1>)/sqrt(2), the bit oracle U_f|x>|-> = (-1)^f(x)|x>|-> acts as a phase oracle on the main register.

questions.question: In Grover's algorithm, a phase oracle is used to mark the solution states.
questions.explanation: True! Grover's algorithm uses a phase oracle that applies a -1 phase to solution states: O_f|x> = (-1)^f(x)|x> where f(x) = 1 for solutions and 0 otherwise.
